skill.name: animation-shader
skill.description: Guides implementation and configuration of animation-style toon shaders, including outlines, cel shading, rim lighting, MatCap, emission, dissolve, hatching, and related VFX.
skill.summary: Configure toon and cel shader effects using curated references for popular Unity shader libraries.
skill.author: Yuki001

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many critical and high-risk patterns, but targeted review found Markdown shader documentation, HLSL pseudocode, shader macro names, and public reference links rather than executable marketplace code. No prompt injection, credential access, command execution, or data exfiltration intent was found in the reviewed files. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk from dense code examples that may continue to trigger automated scanners.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Shader Pseudocode Matched Code Execution Rules
low_findings.description: The reported dynamic function constructor and shell execution alerts are HLSL snippets, shader function labels, macro names, and inline Markdown formatting. They document shader math and do not execute commands or construct runtime code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are documentation for shader functions, macros, or feature mappings. No executable script runtime, user input sink, or command execution API is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Sensitive File and Filesystem Terms Are Shader Tokens
low_findings.description: The sensitive-file and filesystem alerts are caused by shader tokens such as sampler macros and texture map names. The skill contains reference material and does not read local files beyond instructing the assistant to consult bundled Markdown references.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Context shows shader sampler and texture map terminology, not Windows SAM access or memory-mapped file operations. No file IO API or credential path is evidenced.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Public AudioLink URL Is Documentation
low_findings.description: The single hardcoded URL points to the public AudioLink project used by VRChat shaders. It is a documentation reference and there is no network request or data transfer code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears in prose describing an external shader standard. No fetch, webhook, upload, or credential-bearing request is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High Entropy From Multilingual Technical Text
low_findings.description: High-entropy heuristic alerts occur on Markdown reference documents with dense technical shader terminology and multilingual content. Reviewed examples are readable documentation, not encoded payloads or encrypted blobs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sampled high-entropy files are plain Markdown with Chinese and English shader explanations. No obfuscation boundary, decoder, or hidden executable content was found.

content.value_statement: Stylized character shaders require many connected choices across lighting, outlines, textures, and effects. This skill maps visual goals to concrete toon shader features and reference files for faster implementation.
actual_capabilities: Maps visual styles such as anime, manga, cyber, and semi-realistic toon to shader feature sets.
Identifies relevant reference documents for lilToon, Poiyomi, UTS2, RToon, SToon, and ToonShadingCollection.
Explains shader features including outlines, rim light, MatCap, emission, dissolve, hatching, and UV animation.
Provides a standalone workflow for choosing presets, merging feature requirements, and planning implementation.
Supports shader configuration guidance for Unity, VRChat avatar work, and stylized game characters.
limitations: It is a reference skill, not an executable shader package or Unity editor extension.
It does not verify shader compatibility against a specific Unity render pipeline or project version.
It may require reading several bundled reference files before producing a complete recommendation.
It gives implementation guidance but does not replace artist review of final visual quality.

use_cases.title: Plan an Anime Character Shader
use_cases.description: Choose a base anime preset, identify required lighting and outline features, and locate the right implementation references.

use_cases.title: Configure a VRChat Avatar Material
use_cases.description: Map desired glow, AudioLink, dissolve, rim light, and MatCap effects to Poiyomi or lilToon settings.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Stylized Art Direction
use_cases.description: Compare manga, pop art, cyber, retro, and semi-realistic toon feature stacks before building a shader plan.

prompt_templates.title: Pick a Starting Style
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the animation-shader skill to recommend a toon shader preset for a clean anime character. Explain the required features and reference files.

prompt_templates.title: Map Effects to Features
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the animation-shader skill to plan a shader with rim light, MatCap hair shine, and a glowing outline. Include supported shader libraries.

prompt_templates.title: Create a VRChat Shader Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the animation-shader skill to design a Poiyomi or lilToon configuration for a VRChat avatar with emission, AudioLink, and dissolve.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Advanced Pipelines
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the animation-shader skill to compare semi-realistic toon and cyber VFX approaches. Include feature tradeoffs, references, and implementation risks.

output_examples.input: I want a manga-style character shader with visible hand-drawn texture.
output: Core features: desaturated color adjustment, sketchy outline, hatching, halftone overlay, and paper texture overlay.
Suggested references: SToon overlay and outline documents, plus lilToon or Poiyomi color adjustment references.

output_examples.input: Build a cyber anime material with music-reactive glow.
output: Core features: dark base texture, emission, AudioLink modulation, dissolve, vertex glitch, and rim lighting.
Suggested libraries: Poiyomi for AudioLink-heavy avatar work, or lilToon for focused stylized effects.

output_examples.input: Make armor look toon shaded but still metallic.
output: Core features: PBR metallic controls, normal map, shadow ramp, MatCap, and controlled outline width.
Review point: verify the balance between stylized shadow boundaries and material readability in the target lighting.

best_practices: Start with a preset, then add only the specific effects needed for the art direction.
Read every reference file listed for a selected feature before producing implementation guidance.
Validate the final shader look in the target engine, lighting setup, and avatar or character scale.
anti_patterns: Do not mix many shader effects without a clear visual priority or performance budget.
Do not choose a shader library before mapping the requested style to required features.
Do not treat pseudocode references as drop-in production shader code without adaptation.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete shader files?
faq.answer: It primarily provides planning and reference guidance. It can help draft shader code when paired with a coding workflow.

faq.question: Which shader libraries does it cover?
faq.answer: It references lilToon, Poiyomi, UnityChan Toon Shader 2, RToon, SToon, and ToonShadingCollection.

faq.question: Can it help with VRChat avatars?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes avatar-focused features such as Poiyomi, lilToon, AudioLink, emission, dissolve, and MatCap guidance.

faq.question: Is it useful for non-Unity projects?
faq.answer: Yes for visual planning and shader concepts, but implementation details may need translation to the target engine.

faq.question: Does it replace a technical artist?
faq.answer: No. It helps structure choices and references, but final visual quality still needs art direction and testing.

faq.question: Why does the audit mention scanner false positives?
faq.answer: The skill contains many Markdown code examples and shader terms. Automated scanners can mistake these for executable security patterns.
